skill.name: specdd-test
skill.description: Derive, add, update, and assess tests from active SpecDD specification contracts.
skill.summary: Connect SpecDD specification contracts to focused test verification with minimal, behavior-driven test cases.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: The SKILL.md file is a pure documentation skill with no executable code. All 14 external_commands findings are false positives caused by Markdown backtick formatting (e.g., `.specdd/bootstrap.md`, code-like terms such as `Must`, `Scenario`). The weak cryptographic algorithm finding on line 3 is also a false positive — line 3 contains a description string with no cryptographic content. No code execution, network access, file system writes, or credential handling is present.

content.user_title: Derive Targeted Tests From Your SpecDD Specifications
content.value_statement: Specification gaps leave test coverage incomplete and unverified. This skill reads active SpecDD spec chains and produces minimal, behavior-driven tests that cover each required scenario.
actual_capabilities: Read and follow the active SpecDD bootstrap chain (bootstrap.md, bootstrap.project.md, bootstrap.local.md).
Resolve the effective spec chain for a target path, task, or behavior change.
Derive test cases from spec entries labeled Must, Scenario, Handles, Returns, Raises, Tasks, and Done when.
Prefer the smallest tests that prove the specified observable behavior.
Run the project test command when one is available and report results.
Identify spec entries that still lack verification coverage.
limitations: Only works for projects that already have a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file.
Cannot create broad test harnesses unless the spec or project convention requires them.
Asks for confirmation before writing tests when no spec grants clear test-file authority.
Does not generate tests for non-SpecDD projects — use specdd-adopt for that purpose.

use_cases.title: Add Tests for a New Spec Entry
use_cases.description: A developer adds a new Must clause to a SpecDD spec and needs matching test cases generated automatically.

use_cases.title: Audit Test Coverage Against Specs
use_cases.description: A tech lead wants a report of which spec entries have tests and which lack verification coverage.

use_cases.title: Regression Tests for Confirmed Bugs
use_cases.description: A QA engineer confirms a bug and needs a regression test added that locks the fix in place.

prompt_templates.title: Derive Tests for a Single Spec Entry
prompt_templates.prompt: Read .specdd/bootstrap.md and find the spec governing the user login flow. Derive minimal test cases for each Must and Scenario entry in that spec.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with a single target

prompt_templates.title: Audit Coverage for a Module
prompt_templates.prompt: Resolve the spec chain for src/payments/. List every spec entry and whether a test covers it. Flag any spec entry that lacks a corresponding test.

prompt_templates.title: Add Regression Test for a Confirmed Bug
prompt_templates.prompt: A bug was confirmed where the refund endpoint returns 200 instead of 422 for negative amounts. Write a regression test that fails without the fix and passes with it.

prompt_templates.title: Full Chain Walk for a Refactor
prompt_templates.prompt: Walk the full SpecDD spec chain for the order processing module before I refactor it. Tell me every contract entry I must preserve and any test gaps I need to fill.

output_examples.input: Derive tests for the Must: 'Password reset tokens expire after 1 hour' entry in auth.spec.md.
output: Test 1: Token issued now is valid at t+59min — expect 200
Test 2: Token issued now is rejected at t+61min — expect 401
Test 3: Already-expired token in request body returns 401 with expired error code
Coverage note: Must entry is fully covered by 3 tests

output_examples.input: Audit coverage for src/billing/ against all governing SpecDD specs.
output: billing/invoicing.spec.md — 5/6 Must entries covered (gap: late-fee calculation)
billing/subscriptions.spec.md — 2/4 Must entries covered (gap: prorated downgrade, gap: dunning retry)

output_examples.input: Add a regression test for: refund endpoint returns 200 instead of 422 for negative amounts.
output: Test: POST /refunds with amount=-50 returns 422 Unprocessable Entity
Test: POST /refunds with amount=0 returns 422 Unprocessable Entity
Test: POST /refunds with amount=10 returns 200 with refund record
These tests fail on the buggy code and pass once the validation guard is added

best_practices: Always resolve the full spec chain before writing tests to ensure you are testing against the correct governing contract.
Prefer the smallest test that proves the specified behavior — avoid over-testing or testing implementation details.
Cover forbidden behavior entries (negative paths) in addition to happy paths to prevent silent regressions.
anti_patterns: Do not create broad test harnesses or scaffolding when the spec only calls for a single targeted test.
Do not infer write authority from file names, folder structure, or conventions — always check the spec for explicit authority.
Do not rerun the entire bootstrap chain for every small decision — reread only the specific file you need.

faq.question: What is SpecDD?
faq.answer: SpecDD is a specification-driven development methodology where contracts in .specdd/ files govern behavior and grant write authority. This skill is a test-derivation companion for SpecDD projects.

faq.question: Does this skill work on non-SpecDD projects?
faq.answer: No. If .specdd/bootstrap.md is missing, this skill will not continue. Use the specdd-adopt skill to add SpecDD to a new or existing project first.

faq.question: How does the skill handle spec authority?
faq.answer: The skill reads the bootstrap chain in order and follows whichever spec grants write authority for the target. It will not infer authority from filenames or conventions.

faq.question: Can the skill write tests without explicit authority?
faq.answer: No. If a spec requires behavior but grants no clear test-file authority, the skill will ask for confirmation before editing.

faq.question: What test framework does it use?
faq.answer: The skill uses whatever test command the project already provides. It does not impose a framework — it runs the project test command when available.

faq.question: Does it cover both happy paths and edge cases?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill derives tests from all applicable spec entries including Must, Scenario, Handles, Returns, Raises, Tasks, and Done when. It also covers forbidden behavior that could regress silently.
